title: Scam.ai Announces Qualcomm Partnership, Launches Halo Deepfake Detection Model at Computex 2026

raw_body:
New partnership brings on-device deepfake detection to video calls on desktop SAN FRANCISCO, June 29, 2026 — Scam.ai today announced a partnership with Qualcomm and the launch of Halo, an on-device deepfake detection model for live video calls. Both announcements were made at Computex 2026 in Taipei, where Scam.ai was featured at Qualcomm’s booth as […]
The post Scam.ai Announces Qualcomm Partnership, Launches Halo Deepfake Detection Model at Computex 2026 appeared first on AI News.
speedrun: Scam.ai has partnered with Qualcomm to launch Halo, an on-device deepfake detection model aimed at enhancing security during live video calls. This announcement was made at Computex 2026 in Taipei, showcasing Halo's ability to identify manipulated content in real-time. The integration of this technology could significantly improve trust in virtual communications, addressing growing concerns about deepfake misuse.
why_it_matters: Users of video call platforms could benefit from enhanced security, making conversations more trustworthy and reducing the risk of scams.
This partnership signals a shift towards prioritizing digital safety in communication technologies, reflecting a broader trend in the tech industry.

lenses.eli12: Scam.ai's new model, Halo, helps spot fake videos during live calls, like a security guard checking IDs at the door. This is important because it could make online conversations safer and more reliable for everyone, especially as deepfakes become more common.
lenses.pm: For product managers, Halo addresses a growing user need for security in digital communications. By integrating this model, platforms could enhance user trust, potentially leading to increased engagement and lower churn rates as users feel safer during calls.
lenses.engineer: Halo utilizes advanced algorithms for real-time deepfake detection, likely leveraging Qualcomm's processing power for efficient performance. This model could set a new benchmark for on-device AI applications, emphasizing the importance of security in live video interactions.
